title: Resource Access Graph
description: Portfolio access-linkage layer. Maps credentials, secret-store items, access methods, and host posture to the resources they reach. Foreign-keys into infrastructure-catalog (server.id, environment.id, secret.name, objectStorage.bucket) rather than duplicating inventory. The canonical home for 'which key/credential/method reaches which resource, by what route, with what posture' — answers the recurring key-mismatch by making access posture queryable, not rediscovered live.

catalogRef.description: FK target. The infrastructure-catalog instance this access graph links into. All resourceRef values resolve against this catalog.

instancePath.description: Path to the governed catalog instance, e.g. .roadmap/infrastructure-catalog.json

credentialStore.description: Secret-store identity and the item registry. Vault NAME and item IDS only — never values.

activeVault.description: Current canonical vault identity (name/login), e.g. luhtechnology.com

migratingTo.description: Target vault identity if a migration is in progress, else null

doNotUse.description: Vault identities that must never be used for secret ops (e.g. empty/legacy vaults)

items.description: Secret-store item registry. UUID + role only. FK into catalog secret.name via secretRef.

accessMethods.description: Canonical access methods by category. The typed vocabulary of HOW a resource class is reached.

hostPosture.description: Per-host access posture. FK into catalog server.id. Answers the recurring key-mismatch: which keys reach which hosts, and whether the host is SSH-reachable, zero-ssh, or break-glass-only.

envRoutes.description: Atomic env-change routing chains. The ordered FK path a value travels: credential item -> CI secret -> IaC template -> object-store target.

accessGrants.description: The access graph edges. Each grant links a credential item + access method to a target resource. The core FK table.

itemId.description: BW item UUID (identifier only, never the value).

role.description: What this credential is for, e.g. staging-tier2-jwt, spaces-staging, cis-db

secretRef.description: FK -> infrastructure-catalog secret.name, if this item backs a catalogued secret

field.description: Which secret-store field holds the value(s)

trackedByFollowUp.description: FK -> follow-up-register id tracking rotation, if any

itemName.description: BW item name (stable operator-facing label for the UUID).

id.description: Access method id, e.g. am-doctl, am-deploy-agent-hmac, am-boto3, am-ssh, am-do-console

constraints.description: Hard rules, e.g. 'boto3 only - aws-cli and curl-sigv4 broken', 'never SSH - zero-ssh by design'

credentialMethodRef.description: FK -> credentialItem.itemId for the credential this method authenticates with, if any

posture.description: Canonical access posture. zero-ssh = ops via webhook only; key-mismatched = local keys do not match authorized_keys (the recurring failure class)

primaryAccessMethodRef.description: FK -> accessMethod.id. The sanctioned route to operate this host (e.g. am-deploy-agent-hmac for zero-ssh hosts)

breakGlassMethodRef.description: FK -> accessMethod.id. The fallback for direct inspection (e.g. am-do-console)

reachableKeys.description: Key NAMES known to reach this host (operator-facing, e.g. luhtech_enterprise, ectropy-production). Names only — never private key material or fingerprints. Empty for zero-ssh hosts.

atomic.description: If true, all chain stages must land together

chain.description: Ordered FK stages a value travels, source-first

ref.description: FK to the target at this stage: credentialItem.itemId | GH secret name (TF_VAR_*) | terraform template path | environment.objectStorage.bucket | server.id

ephemeral.description: True if this stage is regenerated/overwritten (e.g. config-sync rewrites host-env; TF regenerates .env) - i.e. patching it alone does not persist

resourceRef.description: FK into infrastructure-catalog: server.id | environment.id | database.id | environment.objectStorage.bucket | externalService.id

resourceType.description: Which catalog table resourceRef points into (disambiguates the FK)

accessMethodRef.description: FK -> accessMethod.id used to reach this resource

credentialRef.description: FK -> credentialItem.itemId required for this grant, if any

hostPostureRef.description: FK -> hostPosture.serverRef when resourceType is server
